intro: Guapira harrisiana, also known as "Guapira" or "Yellow Guapira," is a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
